questions.q: By what affectionate short name was C. Rajagopalachari widely known?
questions.explanation: C. Rajagopalachari was popularly known as Rajaji.

questions.q: C. Rajagopalachari holds the distinction of being the first and only Indian to hold which post in independent India?
questions.explanation: Rajaji was the first and only Indian Governor-General of independent India.

questions.q: Rajaji was closely associated with which leader of the Indian freedom movement?
questions.explanation: Rajaji was a senior Congress statesman and close associate of Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.q: In which region of India did Rajaji conduct his portion of the 1930 Salt Satyagraha?
questions.explanation: Rajaji led the Salt Satyagraha in the south of India.

questions.q: Which profession did C. Rajagopalachari practise before entering politics?
questions.explanation: Rajaji trained and practised as a lawyer before devoting himself to the national movement.

questions.q: From which present-day Indian state did C. Rajagopalachari originally hail?
questions.explanation: Rajaji was born in the Tamil-speaking region that is now Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: Rajaji served as Governor-General of India during which broad period of the country's history?
options: Before the founding of the Congress
During the transition to a republic
questions.explanation: He held the office of Governor-General before India became a republic in 1950.

questions.q: The Salt Satyagraha in which Rajaji participated was part of which wider campaign?
questions.explanation: The salt campaigns of 1930 launched the Civil Disobedience Movement.

questions.q: Which political party was Rajaji a senior member of during the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Rajaji was a senior statesman of the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: What kind of historical figure is Rajaji primarily remembered as in the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Rajaji is remembered as a senior statesman and Congress leader.

questions.q: In which year did C. Rajagopalachari assume office as Governor-General of independent India?
questions.explanation: Rajaji became Governor-General in 1948, succeeding Lord Mountbatten.

questions.q: Whom did Rajaji succeed as Governor-General of independent India?
questions.explanation: Rajaji took over from Lord Mountbatten, who had been India's first Governor-General after independence.

questions.q: Rajaji's coastal salt march in the south in 1930 set out for which destination?
questions.explanation: Rajaji led the southern salt march to Vedaranyam on the Coromandel coast.

questions.q: Rajaji served as the Premier (Chief Minister) of which province during 1937-1939?
questions.explanation: Rajaji headed the Congress ministry as Premier of the Madras Presidency from 1937 to 1939.

questions.q: Rajaji's daughter Lakshmi married a son of which national leader, linking the two families?
questions.explanation: Rajaji's daughter married Devdas Gandhi, a son of Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.q: Which conservative political party did Rajaji found in 1959, well after independence?
questions.explanation: Rajaji founded the free-market, conservative Swatantra Party in 1959.

questions.q: Rajaji is known for retellings in English of which two great Indian epics?
questions.explanation: Rajaji wrote widely read English retellings of the Ramayana and the Mahabharata.

questions.q: What high civilian honour was conferred on Rajaji in 1954, making him among its first recipients?
questions.explanation: Rajaji was among the first recipients of the Bharat Ratna in 1954.

questions.q: Rajaji later served as the Chief Minister of which state in the early 1950s?
questions.explanation: Rajaji served as Chief Minister of Madras State in the early 1950s.

questions.q: During the 1942 period Rajaji proposed a controversial formula that broke with much of the Congress mainstream. What did it broadly concern?
options: Joining the Second World War effort fully
Accepting the principle of partition to reach a settlement
questions.explanation: Rajaji controversially argued for conceding the principle of partition to settle the Hindu-Muslim impasse.

questions.q: After Rajaji's term as Governor-General ended, who became the first President of the Republic of India in 1950?
questions.explanation: When India became a republic in 1950 the post of Governor-General lapsed and Rajendra Prasad became the first President.

questions.q: In what year was C. Rajagopalachari born?
questions.explanation: C. Rajagopalachari was born in 1878.

questions.q: On what date did Rajaji formally assume the office of Governor-General of independent India?
questions.explanation: Rajaji took office as Governor-General on 21 June 1948.

questions.q: Rajaji served briefly in 1947 as Governor of which province before becoming Governor-General?
questions.explanation: Rajaji served as Governor of West Bengal in 1947 before his appointment as Governor-General.

questions.q: Which education scheme introduced by Rajaji's Madras government in the early 1950s provoked strong opposition for being seen as caste-based?
options: The Modified Scheme of Elementary Education
questions.explanation: Rajaji's Modified Scheme of Elementary Education drew fierce criticism as caste-perpetuating and was withdrawn.

questions.q: In his anti-nuclear-weapons activism late in life, Rajaji visited which country in 1962 to appeal for disarmament?
questions.explanation: Rajaji led a peace delegation to the United States in 1962 to plead for nuclear disarmament.

questions.q: In which year did C. Rajagopalachari die?
questions.explanation: C. Rajagopalachari died in 1972.

questions.q: Rajaji's English-language daily and writings often appeared in a journal he edited called what?
questions.explanation: Rajaji wrote prolifically and was closely associated with the journal Swarajya.

questions.q: During the Non-Cooperation years Rajaji set up an ashram for constructive work in which Tamil region?
questions.explanation: Rajaji established an ashram at Tiruchengode for khadi and constructive programme work.

questions.q: Rajaji's first major break from the Gandhian Congress mainstream came when he opposed which of the following in the early 1940s?
options: The boycott of the Simon Commission
The decision to launch the Quit India Movement
questions.explanation: Rajaji opposed the launch of the Quit India Movement in 1942, parting from the Congress majority and resigning from the party for a time.
